title: Nevada Rest Break Requirements
summary: Nevada requires employers to provide a paid 10-minute rest break for every 4 hours of work, or major fraction thereof. The rest break must be provided as close to the middle of the 4-hour work period as practicable. Rest breaks are counted as hours worked and must be compensated.

rest_period.frequency: Per 4 hours worked or major fraction thereof
rest_period.timing: As close to the middle of the 4-hour work period as practicable.
rest_period.description: A paid 10-minute rest break must be provided for every 4 hours of work or major fraction thereof. The break should be scheduled near the midpoint of each 4-hour period.

penalties.description: Employers who fail to provide required rest breaks may face enforcement action by the Nevada Labor Commissioner, including fines and orders to comply.

notes: Nevada's rest break requirement is similar to those in California, Colorado, and Washington. The 'major fraction thereof' language means that work periods of more than 2 hours beyond a multiple of 4 trigger an additional rest break. Rest periods are paid time and may not be combined with meal breaks or used to shorten the workday.
